title: Replace full scoreboard with compact games ticker
objective: The Live Games section currently renders large game cards, consuming most of the visible screen. Replace with a compact single-row ticker of score chips so positions are visible without scrolling.
prerequisite: DASH_002 must be complete first (same file — no concurrent edits)
inputs: dashboard.html — games section HTML around line 401-407: <div class='section'>Live Games + <div id='games-list'>
dashboard.html — renderGames() or equivalent JS function that populates #games-list
/api/games endpoint returns: away_team, home_team, away_score, home_score, inning, status, game_id fields
deliverables: Replace the games section div with a compact ticker div: <div id='games-ticker'> with horizontal flex-wrap layout
Each game becomes a small chip: '<abbr> <score> · <abbr> <home_score> (<i{inning}|F|S>)'
Chip background: green-dim for live (inning set), text3 for final/scheduled
Update the JS render function to build chips instead of full cards
Remove the large section-header and section padding around games
acceptance_criteria: Games section occupies a single line (or two lines if many games wrap)
Each chip shows: away abbr, away score, home abbr, home score, status indicator
Live games (with inning) have a subtle green accent
No full game cards with large padding
KPI strip and drawer are immediately visible without scrolling
verification: Open http://localhost:5000 — games should be a compact ticker, not a full scoreboard
